author: dashboard-bootstrap
content: Plan: two-part fix. (1) smoke-verify job needs a docker login step before docker pull — currently only publish-image authenticates with GITHUB_TOKEN, so smoke-verify hits 'unauthorized' against the just-pushed (private-by-default) GHCR package. Adding the same docker/login-action@v3 step that publish-image uses. (2) The deeper issue: GH#576's whole point was 'users can pull without manual auth' — but GHCR creates packages as private on first publish, and the only way to make them public is a manual admin action via the package settings page. CI fix unblocks the workflow; the manual step unblocks the actual user-facing feature. Documenting the manual step in the PR body + adding a CHANGELOG note + considering whether to add a one-time bootstrap script. Need to verify whether linking the package to the repo (which happens automatically on first push) inherits the repo's visibility — I think it does for new packages, but the existing :nightly was published before any visibility setting was made, so it may need explicit toggling.
